reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: My own answer to 37 + 45 is solid, but Q2–Q4 are unverified, so volunteering would risk my life on three unchecked answers. Verifying instead of passing doesn’t change my death risk this round (random death only happens if nobody volunteers) but does cost a coin. In the last several games, someone—usually B—has volunteered in round 1 even without extra verification, and been correct, so the chance that nobody volunteers is low. I’ll conserve coins and pass, avoiding the higher personal risk of volunteering on fully unverified capsules.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (7 × 8) is straightforward, so I’m certain my capsule Q2-B is correct. Every past game’s puzzles have been simple arithmetic with no conflicting capsules when there’s one per question, and volunteering immediately has always led to success, while passing once caused my random death. Verifying would just waste coins and still risk random elimination if no one else volunteers, so I’ll volunteer now using the single available capsule for each question.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: In Round 1, every question has only a single-endorsed capsule, so volunteering would mean staking my life on three unverified answers from others—unnecessary risk when I gain nothing special by going first.  

If no one volunteers, a random player dies whether I pass or verify, so verifying doesn’t reduce my survival risk this round; it only costs me 1 coin. In the last several games, B has consistently volunteered in Round 1 using the initial capsules and succeeded, so it’s very likely someone else will step up and avoid the random elimination. I’ll keep my full 4 coins and pass.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
